stem: A education study measures average test score improvement across 4 schools, each containing multiple classrooms. school A has 20 observations with mean 84.0 points (within-school SD = 3.1). school B has 5 observations with mean 58.6 points (within-school SD = 5.0). school C has 20 observations with mean 73.4 points (within-school SD = 6.4). school D has 10 observations with mean 71.8 points (within-school SD = 6.5). The between-school standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 6.1 and 15.3 points. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true average test score improvement for school C, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-school variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for school C: 73.4 points (shrunk from raw 73.4). Range [73.4, 73.5] as between-school SD varies from 6.1 to 15.3. Pooled mean: 75.6.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 75.6 points (weighted average of 4 school means).
Shrinkage factor for school C: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/20). At σ_between=10.2: B = 0.981.
Interval [73.4, 73.5] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (73.4) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (75.6) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
